reasoning: XLF down 0.9% while XLK flat (+0.08%) reveals selective financial weakness. SPY premarket down 0.27% is led by financial sector pressure, not broad tech selling. Tech resilience (XLK outperforming SPY by +0.35%) suggests the overnight gap-down (-0.59%) is yield/rate driven rather than momentum shift. Single sector weakness (XLF) with others muted = medium-strength bearish signal. Financial internals negative, but breadth not confirming.
key_observations: XLK: +0.08% — tech is holding, not selling. Largest SPY component resilient.
XLF: -0.9% — financials clearly weak, suggesting yield/rate pressure overnight into premarket.
Rotation: selective risk-off into financials only. XLE down 0.4% suggests mild commodity pressure but not broad energy collapse.
Breadth: narrow. XLK outperforming SPY indicates the -0.27% SPY move is financial-led, not tech-led. Limits downside conviction.
